compiled_state.title: As a DSO maintainer, CLAUDE.md and all rule citations reflect usage-aware throttling
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace CLAUDE.md rule #4 text, update template and examples, and update all inline citations of the old rule in skill files
**Why**: The rule is the primary enforcement mechanism for the old cap; stale rule text creates confusion and contradictions
**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md rule #4, templates/CLAUDE.md.template, examples/CLAUDE.md.example, inline rule citations in fix-bug/SKILL.md (lines 976, 984) and any other skill files referencing "5 sub-agents" or "groups of 5"
- OUT: Skill consumer behavior changes (Story 6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, grep for "5 sub-agents" across CLAUDE.md, templates/, and examples/ returns no matches
  ← Satisfies: "CLAUDE.md rule #4 no longer contains the phrase 5 sub-agents"
- When this story is complete, the new rule references orchestration.max_agents config key and the two throttle tiers (90%/95% for 5-hour, 95%/98% for 7-day)
  ← Satisfies: "CLAUDE.md rule #4... references the orchestration.max_agents config key and the two throttle tiers"
- When this story is complete, all inline CLAUDE.md rule #4 citations in skill files (e.g., fix-bug/SKILL.md "(CLAUDE.md rule: never create more than 5 sub-agents at a time)") are updated to reference the new rule text
  ← Satisfies: "CLAUDE.md rule #4... replaced with usage-aware throttling guidance"

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Stale worktree branches will see old rules until rebased — this is acceptable and expected
- [Maintainability] grep -r for "5 sub-agents", "groups of 5", "batch.*5", "batches of 5" to find all references
